activities_and_involvement.school_involvement_include_offices_held_:
I am currently a sophomore Honors student at North Carolina A&T State University majoring in Biology (Pre-Med track). Throughout my first two years, I have intentionally sought out opportunities to lead, serve, and excel academically.

CAMPUS ORGANIZATIONS AND OFFICES HELD
- Minority Association of Pre-Medical Students (MAPS): I have been an active member since Fall 2022. This year (2024–2025), I was elected Corresponding Secretary. In this role, I manage our chapter email account, send weekly newsletters about shadowing opportunities and MCAT prep sessions, and coordinate communication with local physicians who volunteer to speak at our meetings. For example, I recently organized a virtual Q&A session with an A&T alumna who is now a resident in internal medicine at Duke.

- Alpha Lambda Delta Honor Society: I was inducted in Spring 2023 after maintaining above a 3.5 GPA my first year. As a member, I volunteer at our exam prep nights for first-year students. I help lead a biology review station where I share study strategies, old practice questions, and create mini practice quizzes to help them prepare for midterms.

- Honors Program: I am a member of the University Honors Program and participate in honors seminars and service projects. Last semester, I completed an Honors Contract in my Cell Biology course, where I conducted an extended literature review on cardiovascular health disparities in Black communities and presented my findings to my class.

- Residence Hall Association (RHA): During my freshman year, I served as Floor Representative for my residence hall, Pride Hall. In this position, I attended weekly hall council meetings, helped plan stress-relief events during midterms and finals (such as a "Self-Care Sunday" with journaling and mindfulness activities), and gathered feedback from residents to bring to the hall director.

- Biology Club: I have been an active member since my first semester. I regularly attend monthly meetings and have volunteered at club-sponsored events, including tutoring sessions for General Biology I and II and a campus-wide science fair where we hosted interactive demos for local high school students.

HONORS, SCHOLARSHIPS, AND ACADEMIC RECOGNITION
- Dean’s List: I have earned Dean’s List honors every semester since starting at NC A&T (Fall 2022, Spring 2023, Fall 2023, and Spring 2024) for maintaining a GPA above 3.5.

- University Honors Program Scholarship: I currently receive a partial tuition scholarship through the Honors Program based on academic merit and continued participation in honors coursework and community service.

- STEM Excellence Award (Department of Biology): In Spring 2024, I received a departmental recognition certificate for outstanding performance in Organic Chemistry I and Cell Biology, as nominated by my professors. This recognition was based on my final course grades, class participation, and my willingness to help classmates during group activities and labs.

- High School Valedictorian (Background): Although I am now in college, my high school achievements laid the foundation for my current involvement. I graduated as valedictorian from West Charlotte High School in 2022 with a 4.36 weighted GPA. I mention this because the habits I built then—time management, seeking mentoring, and balancing activities—still shape how I show up on campus today.

RESEARCH AND ACADEMIC ENRICHMENT
- Undergraduate Research Assistant (Summer 2024): I was selected to participate in a summer research experience in the Department of Biology, assisting a faculty mentor studying the impact of diet and exercise on blood pressure regulation in African American adults. My responsibilities include entering and cleaning survey data, helping to prepare blood pressure cuffs and materials for participants, and attending weekly lab meetings where we discuss research articles. This experience has deepened my interest in cardiology and preventative medicine.

- Peer Study Group Leader: While this is informal, I organize a weekly study group for students taking Human Anatomy & Physiology. I create shared Google Docs with summarized notes, practice questions, and diagrams. Several students in my group have told me that their quiz grades improved after joining, which has encouraged me to consider becoming a Supplemental Instruction (SI) leader next year.

Overall, my school involvement reflects my commitment to academic excellence, leadership, and preparation for a career in medicine. I am intentional about choosing activities that not only help me grow but also allow me to support my peers.
activities_and_involvement.community_involvement:
Community service has been a consistent part of my life since high school, and I have continued that commitment while in college, especially in ways that connect to health, education, and supporting Black communities.

HEALTH-FOCUSED COMMUNITY SERVICE
- Free Blood Pressure Screenings at Local Church: Through a partnership between NC A&T’s Biology Club and a local church in Greensboro (New Hope Missionary Baptist Church), I volunteer twice a month at their community health outreach events. Under the supervision of a registered nurse, I help check blood pressure, distribute informational pamphlets on hypertension and heart-healthy eating, and explain the numbers to participants in plain language. Many of the older church members are more comfortable with students who look like them, so I try to take extra time to answer questions, encourage follow-up with their primary care providers, and share simple tips like reducing sodium and walking daily.

- Health Fair Volunteer: This past spring, I helped organize and staff a community health fair on campus that was open to residents of Greensboro. My role was to manage the registration table, guide families to different service stations (vision screening, dental information, nutrition counseling), and assist with a children’s area where we taught kids how to build a “healthy plate” using plastic food models. I also helped with setting up and breaking down tables and making sure all participants completed feedback forms so we could improve future events.

YOUTH AND EDUCATION OUTREACH
- Tutoring at Smith Academy Middle School: Once a week during the semester, I volunteer through a campus mentoring program at Smith Academy, a local middle school. I tutor 7th and 8th graders in math and science, focusing on students who have been identified as needing extra support. I work with them on homework, break down concepts step-by-step, and share study tips that helped me when I was their age. I also talk with them about what college is like, what it means to be a STEM major, and how they can start preparing now through their course choices and activities.

- Back-to-School Supply Drives: Every August, my home church in Charlotte, Greater Faith Christian Center, hosts a back-to-school drive. I help sort and pack backpacks with notebooks, pencils, folders, and hygiene items. I also help on distribution day, greeting families, checking off names on the registration list, and handing out supplies. One year, we ran short on backpacks, so I organized a small online fundraiser among my friends and family to purchase an additional 25 backpacks so no child had to be turned away.

- Reading Buddy Program: During winter and spring breaks when I am home in Charlotte, I volunteer at the public library’s Reading Buddy program. I sit with elementary school children and read picture books and early chapter books with them, asking questions to check their understanding and encourage them to talk about the stories. As someone who loved reading growing up, it means a lot to me to help younger kids build confidence and see reading as enjoyable rather than just schoolwork.

SERVICE THROUGH FAITH COMMUNITY
- Youth Ministry Volunteer: At my home church, I assist with the youth ministry when I am home on breaks. I help plan lesson activities, lead small group discussions for high school girls about topics like self-esteem, goal-setting, and healthy friendships, and chaperone outings such as college tours and community service projects. I share my own experiences about transitioning from high school to college, managing stress, and staying grounded spiritually.

- Food Pantry Assistance: Our church also runs a small monthly food pantry. When I am in town, I help unload deliveries, sort canned goods, organize fresh produce, and pack boxes for families. I have learned a lot about food insecurity and how something as simple as offering fresh fruits and vegetables can make a real difference for families who might otherwise rely on convenience foods.

OTHER COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT
- Voter Registration Drives: In Fall 2022 and 2023, I volunteered with a nonpartisan student organization that hosted voter registration tables on campus and at a local community center. I helped people check their registration status, fill out forms, and find their polling locations. We emphasized the importance of local elections, especially for issues like school funding and public health.

- Neighborhood Clean-Ups: Through both NC A&T and my church in Charlotte, I have participated in several neighborhood clean-up days. We pick up trash, clear debris from sidewalks, and sometimes help elderly residents with yard work. While this seems simple, it has made me more aware of environmental health and how our physical surroundings impact our sense of pride and well-being.

Overall, my community involvement is driven by my desire to become a physician who not only treats illness but also understands the broader social and community factors that affect health. I try to choose service opportunities where I can build relationships, listen to people’s stories, and offer practical support—whether that is tutoring a middle school student, checking someone’s blood pressure, or simply making sure a child starts the school year with the supplies they need.
